title: buildah-from with ONBUILD and --quiet: is not quiet
body:
This seems to be an undefined corner case:
```console
# cat Dockerfile
FROM alpine
ONBUILD RUN date
# buildah bud --format=docker --quiet -t foo .
baa08aa3383dc647dd543aed0c5d2e8bae1d7a63b060e869dbc68524ea862ee1
# buildah from --quiet foo
STEP 1: RUN date                 <<< unexpected
Tue Dec 10 21:56:15 UTC 2019     <<< unexpected
foo-working-container            <<< expected
```
Note that there are two different unexpected lines in the output: the `STEP` one, and the actual output of the command itself.

`buildah-from(1)` describes `--quiet` as follows:
```
   --quiet, -q

   If an image needs to be pulled from  the  registry,  suppress  progress output.
```
...so technically speaking the `ONBUILD` behavior is undefined; but I think one can have a reasonable expectation that `--quiet` from `bud` will carry over onto `from`. Plus, docker (19.0.3 - I can't get a RHEL7 virt to test 13.c) seems to respect `--quiet`:
```console
# docker build --quiet .
sha256:7634f230b6506afeb29e8dbfce059fecfdb7f71ce50ff19dcf43f676ad81ce79
```

comments.body: @TomSweeneyRedHat this can be closed. Retested it seemed to work fine.
